title: Lab 19: Login Greeting Messages
description: Configure both a user-specific and a global login greeting with clearer host distribution.

tasks: On server, configure a login message for user orien19 that says: Welcome to you, user Orien, you are amazing!
Configure a global login message on both client and server so any user receives: Welcome [username], you are logged in! with the actual login name.
hints: Per-user shell startup content belongs in the user home.
A profile.d script is the cleanest way to apply the same greeting globally.
checks: test -f /etc/profile.d/lab19-greeting.sh && grep -Fq "Welcome ${USER}, you are logged in!" /etc/profile.d/lab19-greeting.sh
# server test -f /etc/profile.d/lab19-greeting.sh && grep -Fq "Welcome ${USER}, you are logged in!" /etc/profile.d/lab19-greeting.sh
# server test -f /home/orien19/.bash_profile && grep -Fq "Welcome to you, user Orien, you are amazing!" /home/orien19/.bash_profile
solution_outline: Create orien19 on server and add the user-specific message.
Deploy the same profile.d greeting script on both hosts.
task_titles: Create the per-user greeting on server
Create the global login greeting on both systems
solution_commands: echo 'echo "Welcome to you, user Orien, you are amazing!"' >> /home/orien19/.bash_profile
